title: actaea americana var. rubra
intro: Actaea americana var. rubra, also known as American red cohosh, is a variety of the Actaea racemosa plant, native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it useful for treating menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and arthritis. The plant is rich in salicylic acid, which provides pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory benefits, and its extracts have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of American red cohosh include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to support women's health and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause and PMS.
